description: Additional Ctrl Key Bindings - Add C-h (backspace), C-j (enter), and C-l (goto-line) keybindings

userStories.title: C-h as Backspace in all modes
userStories.description: As a user, I want C-h to delete the character before the cursor so that I can backspace without leaving the home row.
acceptanceCriteria: In normal editing mode, C-h (term.KeyCtrlH) behaves identically to Backspace: calls buf.SaveUndo() then buf.Backspace()
In search mode, C-h removes the last character from the search query (add term.KeyCtrlH to the existing Backspace case)
In minibuffer mode, C-h removes the last character from the minibuffer input (add term.KeyCtrlH to the existing Backspace case)
go build . succeeds
go test ./... passes
userStories.notes: Simple change: add term.KeyCtrlH to every existing 'case term.KeyBackspace, term.KeyBackspace2:' in main.go. There are three locations: search mode handler, minibuffer mode handler, and normal mode handler.

userStories.title: C-j as Enter in all modes
userStories.description: As a user, I want C-j to insert a newline so that I can press Enter without leaving the home row.
acceptanceCriteria: In normal editing mode, C-j (term.KeyCtrlJ) behaves identically to Enter: handles Buffer List special case, otherwise calls buf.SaveUndo() then buf.InsertNewline()
In search mode, C-j exits search and accepts the current match (add term.KeyCtrlJ to the existing Enter case)
In minibuffer mode, C-j accepts the input and calls the callback (add term.KeyCtrlJ to the existing Enter case)
In confirm mode, C-j does NOT act as 'yes' — only 'y'/'n' rune keys are valid (no change needed, confirm mode checks ev.Key() == term.KeyRune)
go build . succeeds
go test ./... passes
userStories.notes: Simple change: add term.KeyCtrlJ to every existing 'case term.KeyEnter:' in main.go. There are three locations: search mode handler, minibuffer mode handler, and normal mode handler. Confirm mode already only responds to KeyRune 'y'/'n', so no change needed there.

userStories.title: C-l Goto Line via minibuffer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to press C-l to be prompted for a line number and jump directly to that line, so that I can navigate large files quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: C-l (term.KeyCtrlL) in normal mode activates the minibuffer with prompt 'Goto line: '
Entering a valid number (e.g. '42') moves cursor to that line (1-based: line 1 = first line), with CursorC set to 0
If the number exceeds total lines, cursor moves to the last line
If the number is less than 1 or not a valid integer, message 'Invalid line number' is shown and cursor does not move
After jumping, message line shows 'Line N' where N is the 1-based line number jumped to
Window scroll adjusts to make target line visible (via existing AdjustScroll in redraw)
C-g cancels the goto-line prompt without moving the cursor
Uses strconv.Atoi to parse input; strconv must be imported if not already
go build . succeeds
go test ./... passes
userStories.notes: Add a new case term.KeyCtrlL in the normal mode key handler. Use the existing minibuffer infrastructure: set minibufferMode=true, minibufferPrompt='Goto line: ', minibufferInput=nil, and minibufferCallback to a closure that parses the input with strconv.Atoi, validates, sets buf.CursorR and buf.CursorC=0. The closure captures buf. AdjustScroll happens automatically in redraw().
